session.name: Cubric Vision foundation brand identity brainstorming

goal.original: Address the Cubric Vision Foundation brand identity kanban entry through brainstorming/cooperation, including prompts for image/design tools.
goal.summary: Naming and accent colors are locked, mascot direction is selected, and external mascot assets are reportedly prepared. The next active topic is lettering: evaluate bold font-based wordmarks for Cubric Studio and app lockups instead of PNG lettering.

completed: Captured product family naming: Cubric Studio as master hub, apps as Cubric Vision/Prompt/Audio/Video.
Captured selected app accents with OKLCH and HEX approximations.
Captured mascot direction: Studio coordinator face, app-specific specialized heads, head-only OS icons, full-body in-app animation variants.
Created and updated a standalone accent swatch board at docs/plans/2026-05-19-cubric-accent-swatch-board.html.
pending: Continue lettering/font exploration and decide whether to replace image lettering with a font-based lockup system.
Create canonical naming table with app ids and subdomains.
Finish mascot scope and lettering treatment decisions in the plan.
Inventory app/website/docs brand text and assets, then classify release-blocking vs non-blocking rows.

allowed_actions: do not perform app rename implementation unless explicitly asked

completed: Updated docs/plans/2026-05-19-cubric-vision-foundation-brand-identity.md with accent HEX values, mascot direction, external asset folder, and lettering direction.
Updated docs/plans/2026-05-19-cubric-accent-swatch-board.html with selected accents and Stage surface context.
pending: If new brand decisions become stable, update the active plan immediately.
Do not update .claude/rules/ without explicit approval.
If final mascot assets are to enter the app, remember the dual-tree asset rule: media/assets sources and runtime assets/mascot plus assets/lettering must mirror intentionally.

next_action.description: Help the user compare bold Google Fonts candidates for a font-based Cubric Studio wordmark, ideally by creating or guiding a side-by-side preview spelling 'Cubric Studio' and later app lockups.

key_decisions: Cubric Studio is the master hub for all Cubric applications.
App names follow Cubric <App>: Cubric Vision, Cubric Prompt, Cubric Audio, Cubric Video, and future Cubric apps.
Selected accents: Studio #C1B6A4, Vision #FC77AA, Prompt #EDE367, Audio #70E2C5, Video #FF9360.
Shared UI surfaces should remain Stage warm mauve; app accents are small, high-contrast identity signals.
Mascot system should be an operator family: Studio has the only normal face; Vision camera/lens, Prompt terminal/speech, Audio waveform/headphones, Video film/playhead.
OS/taskbar icons should be head-only. Full-body mascot variants are for in-app animation and states.
User prefers nailing brand identity even if it delays launch.
constraints: No code rename in the brand-identity child plan; app-rename consumes the decisions and inventory later.
Website push gate still applies: do not push Website until app is downloadable, user review is done, and mascot/logo recolor is landed.
Photoshop does not accept OKLCH directly; use HEX conversions for raster work.
Nano Banana blue backgrounds caused blue fringing; prefer final Cubric deep canvas #31252A or a carefully handled flat background for future cutouts.
Current app Stage deep canvas token is approximately #31252A from oklch(0.28 0.020 350).

recent_events.note: Brand identity and app rename work are completed and reconciled in the umbrella plan and kanban. Do not resume this stale handoff for current foundation work.

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/d6cd2bcd-cd6a-4310-b65b-870397be4a5b.json and use /mpi-kanban:mpi-continue to continue the kanban entry "Cubric Vision foundation - brand-identity". You are continuing brand identity work, not app rename implementation. The next action is to help compare bold font-based lettering options for Cubric Studio and eventual Cubric Vision/Prompt/Audio/Video lockups.
